behavior_summary: This script controls a single guardsman NPC. It provides an inspect/"U8P_GUARD6::look" response, a talk/"U8P_GUARD6::use" conversation with a 4-option menu, area-entry behavior to initialize or revive the guard, and a scheduled routine that can revive/reset the NPC.
U8P_GUARD6::look: when the player inspects the NPC, the script checks Npc::isDead(this) and barks either "dead guardsman" (if dead) or "guardsman" (if alive).

U8P_GUARD6::enterFastArea: triggered when an actor enters the NPC's fast-trigger region. If the NPC is dead the script clears the dead/combat state and teleports it to fixed coordinates; if alive it calls two engine methods (METHOD::func091C then METHOD::func15E7) with suspend points to run area-entry initialization/AI behavior.

U8P_GUARD6::schedule: sets scheduling parameters, invokes an engine scheduling call (METHOD::func143A) and, if process_result() is true, clears the dead state (Npc::clrDead) and calls METHOD::func133F — used for timed respawn/reset behavior.

U8P_GUARD6::use: opens a conversation, performs housekeeping (spawn/ FREE::func1C19, FREE::resetRef), optionally takes an immediate alarm branch if multiple global "free/heart" flags are set, otherwise presents a menu loop (Who are you?, What is your duty?, Where am I?, Bye). It updates script flags guard6Met, guard6Pissed and guard6Thanked and removes menu options as they are selected.
notable_control_flow: Inspect flow (U8P_GUARD6::look) is a simple single condition: if Npc::isDead(this) -> bark 'dead guardsman' else -> bark 'guardsman'.

Area entry (U8P_GUARD6::enterFastArea) branches on Npc::isDead(this): dead -> revive (clrDead, clrInCombat) and teleport to map/coords; alive -> call METHOD::func091C, suspend, then METHOD::func15E7, suspend.

Schedule flow (U8P_GUARD6::schedule) initializes timer parameters, calls METHOD::func143A and suspends; after resume it checks process_result() and on success clears dead state and calls METHOD::func133F.

Conversation loop (U8P_GUARD6::use) initializes a menu array (local2), captures choice into local1, uses strcmp-style branching to handle each option, removes used options with MenuRemove, and uses EndConversation and suspend nodes for early exits and pacing.
open_questions: Exact runtime semantics and side effects of engine methods METHOD::func091C, METHOD::func15E7, METHOD::func143A and METHOD::func133F are unknown — they appear to be initialization/scheduling hooks but their precise effects are inferred (low confidence).

The meaning of the numeric teleport parameters (mapId 0x29, unknown 0x00, x 0x64, y 0x64) is assumed to be spawn coordinates and orientation but the target location's in-game label is not present in the script (medium confidence).
FREE::func1C19 usage during conversation entry appears related to spawn/free housekeeping; its exact resource semantics and the significance of the passed arguments remain unclear (low confidence).
Timing and frequency of U8P_GUARD6::schedule invocation (what triggers the U8P_GUARD6::schedule process) is not visible in the snippet — inferred to be engine-driven (low confidence).

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD6::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short inspect/description response for the NPC; report whether the guard is dead or alive.
entry_conditions: Triggered by player inspect/U8P_GUARD6::look action against the NPC (process type 0x0208).
Reads the NPC runtime state via Npc::isDead(this) to select the appropriate bark.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD6::look
function_map.role: inspect/U8P_GUARD6::look handler (short bark depending on dead/alive)

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD6::enterFastArea
function_map.role: fast-area entry handler: revive/teleport dead NPC or run area-init methods when alive

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD6::schedule
function_map.role: scheduled respawn/reset: set timing params, invoke engine scheduling, clear dead on success

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD6::use
function_map.role: talk-to / conversation handler with menu loop and script-flag management

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: engine query for NPC dead state

function_map.name: Npc::clrDead
function_map.role: engine call to clear NPC dead state (revive)

function_map.name: Npc::clrInCombat
function_map.role: engine call to clear NPC in-combat flag

function_map.name: Npc::teleport
function_map.role: engine call to move NPC to specific map/coords/orientation

external_calls.purpose: Determine whether the NPC is dead to choose revive or initialization path.
Clear the NPC dead flag to revive the guardsman when entering area while dead.
Ensure NPC is not marked in combat after revival.
Teleport the revived NPC to fixed map coordinates (mapId=0x29, 0x00, x=0x64, y=0x64).
Call an engine initialization/AI method during area entry when NPC is alive (inferred).
Call a follow-up engine method after func091C during area entry (inferred).
Initiate or enqueue a scheduled engine/process action using prepared parameters (inferred scheduling kickoff).
Check outcome of the scheduled engine call; drives revive/finalize branch.
Clear dead state when U8P_GUARD6::schedule's process_result() returns true (revive via scheduler).
Finalize or reactivate NPC after successful scheduled revive (inferred).
Housekeeping/spawn/free resource related to conversation entry (inferred).
Reset/clear a FREE reference during conversation setup (inferred housekeeping).

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Counts toward an alarm/"dire" branch at conversation start; each true increments a local counter used to possibly skip to an urgent dialog and end the conversation.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Counts toward the same alarm/"dire" branching decision in U8P_GUARD6::use.

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Directly influences the alarm/"dire" branch (also increments the counter); if true it can force an urgent reply and exit conversation.

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Selects alternate dialogue lines for 'What is your duty?' and 'Where am I?' branches (toggles references to Devon vs Ladyship).

reads.name: guard6Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Determines whether the guard uses an annoyed greeting; also cleared in some dialog branches to calm the NPC.

reads.name: guard6Thanked
reads.likely_effect: Checked on 'Bye' to decide whether to play a one-time thank-you line (prevents repeat replies).

writes.name: guard6Met
writes.likely_effect: Set when the player first talks to this guard to alter subsequent greeting/branching (marks 'met' state).

writes.name: guard6Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Set when the guard becomes annoyed by certain player interactions (e.g., specific branches); used to make the guard respond angrily until cleared elsewhere.

writes.name: guard6Thanked
writes.likely_effect: Set when the guard thanks the player (on Bye under devonInRule) to prevent repeating the thank-you line.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script controls a single guardsman NPC. It provides an inspect/"U8P_GUARD6::look" response, a talk/"U8P_GUARD6::use" conversation with a 4-option menu, area-entry behavior to initialize or revive the guard, and a scheduled routine that can revive/reset the NPC.
